name: Potato Soup with Kale and Bacon
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the potatoes and put them in a pot with cold, salted water to cover. Bring the water to a boil over high heat.
2. Lower the heat and simmer until the potatoes are tender, about 25 minutes. Drain the potatoes, reserving the cooking liquid.
3. Mash the potatoes using a potato masher until they are quite lumpy, not smooth.
4. Cook the bacon over low heat until crisp, turning often. Drain and chop into 1/2 inch chunks.
5. Reserve half of the bacon fat in a separate container. Chop the remaining kale and mince the garlic.
6. In a frying pan, cook half of the chopped kale and minced garlic with salt and pepper until the kale is wilted, about 1 minute. Transfer to a bowl.
7. Repeat steps 4-6 using more bacon fat as needed for the second batch of kale.
8. Return all the cooked kale (including the bacon) to the frying pan. Add the heavy cream and simmer over very low heat until the kale is tender, about 10 minutes.
9. Combine the mashed potatoes with about 3 cups of the reserved potato cooking liquid and 1 cup of chicken stock in a large pot. Adjust the amount of stock as needed for desired consistency.
10. Stir in the cooked kale mixture (with bacon) into the pot. Bring to a simmer and season with salt, pepper, and balsamic vinegar to taste.
11. Garnish the soup with minced chives or green onions before serving.
This recipe is perfect for enjoying a warm German Potato Salad-like experience during rainy but warm weather in Vancouver.
